english: I. pf. kol (khol), cf. skól-ba (skol ba), to boil, to be boiling, ču kol (chu khol) the water is boiling; °kól ǰúg-pa ('khol 'jug pa) to make boil, to set to the fire for boiling, = skól-ba (skol ba); to ferment (dough), to effervesce; to sparkle (beer) W. — II. pf. bkol (bkol), imp. kol (khol) 1. to oblige a person to be a servant or bondman, to use as a servant; in full: bran-du (bran du), also ɣyóg-tu (g.yog tu), °kól-ba ('khol ba); therefore bran-°kól (bran 'khol), kól-po (khol po) servant, man-servant: bkol-spyód-kyi sdug-bsṅál (bkol spyod kyi sdug bsngal) the calamity of servitude, current expression for designating the lot of animals; ɣźán-dag-gis dbáṅ-med-par bkól-ba (gzhan dag gis dbang med par bkol ba) to be enslaved by others, to be compelled to slave-work Thgy.; dgé-baí lás-la bkól-ba (dge ba'i las la bkol ba) to make a person minister to works of virtue. — 2. to save, to spare, to enjoy with moderation Cs., zúr-du ... bkól-ba (zur du ... bkol ba) Lex.; Sch.: saved, laid up, put by. — 3. Sch.: to become insensible, to be asleep, to get benumbed, in reference to the limbs; seems to be used in Med.

1. C. also °kós-ka ('khos ka) (cognate to °kó-ba ('kho ba)), worth, value, importance Cs.; °kós-ćan ('khos can) important, mighty, of great influence, °kós-méd ('khos med) the opposite of it. — 2. °kós-su-páb-pa ('khos su pab pa) clyster Sch.; one Lex. has mkós-púb (mkos pub), w.e.

1. to freeze (of water, earth, provisions); to coagulate, congeal (melted fat etc.). — 2. to feel cold, °kyágs-na gós gyon ('khyags na gos gyon) if you feel cold, put on clothes Glr., *kyag ǰhuṅ* (*khyag byung*) C., *°kyágs-sa rag* (*'khyags sa rag*) W. I feel cold; °kyágs-gri ('khyags gri) a feeling cold, a shivering (cf. ltógs-gri (ltogs gri)) Mil.; *kyágs-ša* (*khyags sha*) W. id., the cold fit of the ague. — °kyags-rúm ('khyags rum), °kyags-róm ('khyags rom) ice Glr.; °kyags-lhám ('khyags lham) snow-shoe Sch.

to run about, to wander, e.g. čá-med yúl-du (cha med yul du) in an unknown country Glr.; °kór-bar ('khor bar) in the orb or round of transmigration, v. °kór-ba ('khor ba); °kyám-ste nor mi bdóg-pa źig ('khyam ste nor mi bdog pa zhig) one who lives as a vagabond Dzl.; dé-dag-ni rnám-par °kyám-pao (de dag ni rnam par 'khyam pa'o) they are mistaken, on the wrong track Wdn.; °kyám-dù ǰúg-pa ('khyam du 'jug pa) to cause to ramble or rove about, to scatter; °kyáms-pa ('khyams pa) strayed, lost, wandering, vagrant; erroneous, erring Tar. 153, 15. — °kyám-°kyi ('khyam 'khyi) a vagrant dog. — °kyáms-po ('khyams po) 1. a vagabond. — 2. n. of a disease Med. — 3. erroneous Tar. — ču °kyám-pa (chu 'khyam pa) inundation, flood Ma.

to err, to go astray, to deviate from, *yan °kyár-la ma ča* (*yan 'khyar la ma cha*) Ld. do not step out of your rank! do not absent yourself! °kyar ('khyar) dogs yod one should be afraid of going astray Thgy.; dpe °kyár-po (dpe 'khyar po) a defective simile; tsig °kyár-po (tshig 'khyar po) an inadequate designation. — In Tar. 48, 4 dpe °kyár-po (dpe 'khyar po) is translated 'epitome' by Schf., but the whole passage is somewhat obscure.
Sch., prob. an incorrect reading for °kyil-ba ('khyil ba).
pf. bkyigs (bkyigs), fut. bkyig (bkyig), to bind (a prisoner, a bundle of straw etc.); *kye* (*khye*) (v. ske (ske)) *kyig-pa* (*khyig pa*) C. to strangle, suffocate.
to comprise, encompass Pth., v. páṅ-pa (pang pa).
Sch.: mig °kyíd-pa (mig 'khyid pa) to turn or roll one's eyes.

1. to be encircled with a halo, as the sun and moon sometimes are Cs.; °oɣ-°kyíms ('og 'khyims) Lex. = परिधि halo; also ǰa-ód °kyíms ('ja' 'od 'khyims) a rainbow-coloured halo appears Pth. — 2.: *na-bún* (*na bun*) or *múg-pa kyíms* (*mug pa khyims*), dúd-pa kyíms* (dud pa khyims*), fog, smoke, comes floating on.
to turn round in a circular course Cs., Lex.: ɣdugs °kyír-ba (gdugs 'khyir ba) to turn a parasol round (?).

vb. n. 1. to wind, to twist; duṅ ɣyás-su °kyíl-ba (dung g.yas su 'khyil ba) a triton or trumpet-shell, wound to the right, and then considered particularly valuable, these shells generally being wound to the left; of the hair: gyén-la °kyíl-ba (gyen la 'khyil ba) wound or twisted (on the crown of the head) Glr.; ro-smád °brúl-du °kyíl-ba (ro smad 'brul du 'khyil ba) the lower part of the body being wound into a serpent (the usual manner of representing the 'klu' (klu)); ód-du °kyíl-źiṅ ('od du 'khyil zhing), the body enveloping itself in light Mil.; to roll: nya ɣser-míg °kyíl- (nya gser mig 'khyil)
